---
title: "Parameter Fitting"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
# background cleaning 
rm(list = ls())


library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)

# bin size 
m <- 57  # 57 size classes 
hours <- 24

#minimum cell volume 
v_min <- 2^(-5)
delta_v <- 0.125 #spacing between size classes 
size_index <- 1:m 
v_i <- v_min * 2^((size_index - 1) * delta_v) #  this vector contains cell volume for actual size classes i = 1,2,3,....,57 .

# True parameters (from Sosik)
gamma_max_true <- 0.20 # max growth fraction 
delta_max_true <- 0.03 # max division fraction 
E_star_true <- 500 # Light intensity 
a_true <- 2 # it controls the size where divisions becomes likely 
b_true <- 2 # ut controls how sharply division increases with cell size 



# Light function - Light level at time t 
# This function helps us create the mock model 

E_t <- function(t) {
  1000 * max(0, sin(2*pi * t / 24))
}

# Growth function - eq 14. sosik 
gamma_t <- function(t, gamma_max, E_star) {
  gamma_max * (1 - exp(-E_t(t) / E_star))
}

# Division function eq 13. sosik
delta_i_t <- function(t, delta_max, a, b) {
  
  if (t < 6) { #t_d = 6 , before hour 6 the probability of division is 0.
    return(rep(0, m))
  }
  
  delta_i <- delta_max * ((a * v_i)^b / (1 + (a * v_i)^b))
  return(delta_i)
}


  # Creating Matrix A(t)

matrix_A <- function(t, gamma_max, delta_max, E_star, a, b) {  # these are some parameters 
  
  A <- matrix(0, nrow = m, ncol = m)

# creating gemma and delta ( growth and division fucntions
  gamma <- gamma_t(t, gamma_max, E_star)
  delta_i <- delta_i_t(t, delta_max, a, b)

  # defining j from sosik eq 10
  j <- 1 + (1 / delta_v)
  
  # Growth - sub diagonal to A corresponding to growth 
  for (i in 1:(m - 1)) {
    A[i + 1, i] <- gamma * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  
  
  
  #  First row of A corresponding to Division
  for (i in 2:(j - 1)) {
    A[1, i] <- A[1, i] + 2 * delta_i[i]
  }

# upper diagonal of A corresponding to division 
  for (i in j:m) {
    smaller_class <- i + 1 - j
    A[smaller_class, i] <- A[smaller_class, i] + 2 * delta_i[i]
  }

# element of A corresponding to smallest size class 
  # Stasis
  A[1, 1] <- A[1, 1] + ((1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[1])) + 2 * delta_i[1]

#diagonal of A corresponding to stasis 
  for (i in 2:m) {
    A[i, i] <- A[i, i] + (1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  
  return(A)
}


# Matrix B(t)
# 1 hour = 6 ten-minute A matrices


matrix_B <- function(t, gamma_max, delta_max, E_star, a, b, dt = 1/6) {
  
  B <- diag(m)        # creates 57X57 identity matrix - we need it because we will do matrix                            multiplication 
  n_steps <- 1 / dt
  
  for (k in 0:(n_steps - 1)) {            # for k in 0:5
    
    time_now <- t + k * dt
    
    A_k <- matrix_A(
      t = time_now,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b
    )
    
    B <- A_k %*% B         #B = A_3 %*% A_2 %*% A_1 , initially B = I , B = A_0 %*% I
  }
  
  return(B)
}


# Initial distribution w(0)

w0 <- dnorm(size_index, mean = 20, sd= 6) # at time 0, most cells are around size class 20.
w0 <- w0 / sum(w0)
plot(v_i,w0)

#  true model distribution


W_true <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)
W_true[1, ] <- w0 # puts the initial distribution into the first row.

for (t in 1:(hours - 1)) {
  
  B_t <- matrix_B(
    t = t,
    gamma_max = gamma_max_true,
    delta_max = delta_max_true,
    E_star = E_star_true,
    a = a_true,
    b = b_true
  )
  
  W_true[t + 1, ] <- B_t %*% W_true[t, ] #w(t+1) = B(t) w(t)
  W_true[t + 1, ] <- W_true[t + 1, ] / sum(W_true[t + 1, ])
}

set.seed(123)

N <- 1000000 #create fake observation 
Counts <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)

for (t in 1:hours) {
  Counts[t, ] <- rmultinom(
    n = 1,
    size = N,
    prob = W_true[t, ]
  )
}

W_obs <- Counts / rowSums(Counts) # fake noisy

# Model for guessed parameters


guess_model <- function(params,W) { #params = c(gamma_max, delta_max, E_star, a, b)
  
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]
  
  W_pred <- matrix(0, nrow = hours, ncol = m)
  W_pred[1, ] <- W[1,]
  
  for (t in 1:(hours - 1)) {
    
    B_t <- matrix_B( # hourly matrix 
      t = t,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b
    )
    
    W_pred[t + 1, ] <- B_t %*% W[t, ] #next step distribution # W(t+1) = B(t)w(t)
    W_pred[t + 1, ] <- W_pred[t + 1, ] / sum(W_pred[t + 1, ])
  }
  
  return(W_pred)
}


# Objective function


objective_function <- function(params,W) { # finds out how bad the guess is 
  
  gamma_max <- params[1]
  delta_max <- params[2]
  E_star <- params[3]
  a <- params[4]
  b <- params[5]
  
  if (gamma_max < 0 || gamma_max > 0.5 ||
      delta_max < 0 || delta_max > 0.5 ||
      E_star <= 0 ||
      a <= 0 ||
      b <= 0) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  W_pred <- guess_model(params, W)
  error <- sum((W - W_pred)^2) # observed and predicted distributions.
  
  return(error)
}

# Fit parameters


initial_guess <- c(0.20, 0.03, 500, 2, 2)

fit <- optim( # objective function as small as possible 
  par = initial_guess,
  fn = objective_function,
  W = W_obs,
  method = "L-BFGS-B", #allows lower and upper bounds.
  lower = c(0, 0, 100, 0.1, 0.1),
  upper = c(0.5, 0.15, 5000, 30, 10)
)

estimated_params <- fit$par #saves the best parameters found by optim.



results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c("gamma_max", "delta_max", "E_star", "a", "b"),
  True = c(gamma_max_true, delta_max_true, E_star_true, a_true, b_true),
  Estimated = estimated_params
)

print(results) 

# Heatmap function

plot_heatmap <- function(W, title_name) {
  
  df <- melt(W)
  colnames(df) <- c("Hour", "SizeClass", "Proportion")
  
  df$Hour <- df$Hour - 1
  df$CellVolume <- v_i[df$SizeClass]
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = CellVolume, y = Hour, fill = Proportion)) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_x_log10() +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "red")) +
    labs(
      title = title_name,
      x = expression("Cell volume ("*mu*m^3*")"),
      y = "Hour after dawn",
      fill = "Proportion"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}


# Plot


W_fit <- guess_model(estimated_params, W = W_obs7)

p_true <- plot_heatmap(W_true, "True Model Distribution")
p_obs  <- plot_heatmap(W_obs, "Fake Observed Distribution")
p_fit  <- plot_heatmap(W_fit, "Fitted Model Distribution")

print(p_true)
print(p_obs)
print(p_fit)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
